obligations.reason: The scope contains a dedicated zeroriscy_debug_unit and core/controller/register-file modules. Debug logic is a high-risk alternate subject only if it reaches protected core state without mediation, so visible RTL should be checked for the actual path and guard coverage.

obligations.operation: halt/resume/read/write/redirect or otherwise control core execution state
obligations.object: core execution state, register-file state, debug-visible control/status state
obligations.expected_guard: debug authentication/authorization or explicit debug-mode entry guard before debug requests can halt, inspect, or alter core state
state_conditions_to_check: reset state of debug enable/authentication state
debug request accepted while not in debug mode
debug exit/resume clears or preserves debug-only controls
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer does not include signal-level debug port list; inspect module internals and core instantiation wiring.

obligations.reason: The scope includes a CSR register block with multiple decode cases and decoder/ID-stage CSR controls. Permission analysis should verify whether protected CSRs are mediated by privilege/access checks rather than merely address-decoded.
obligations.subject: software running on zero-riscy core via CSR instructions
obligations.object: control/status registers affecting privilege, interrupts, trap vectors, debug state, and hardware loop/control state
obligations.expected_guard: machine/privilege-mode and CSR access permission checks, including read-only/write-restricted CSR enforcement
obligations.candidate_path: CSR decode/write path in zeroriscy_cs_registers and instruction decode/ID stage CSR controls
state_conditions_to_check: write attempts to read-only or restricted CSRs
simultaneous hardware update and software write priority
obligations.uncertainty: Zero-riscy configuration may omit full privilege modes; expected guard should be matched to implemented ISA/configuration.

obligations.reason: Visible modules include a load-store unit and fc_demux instances for core data/instruction buses. Obligation is to verify whether address decoding and any security attributes prevent unprivileged or unintended accesses to protected regions.
obligations.subject: zero-riscy core data load/store requester
obligations.object: memory regions, APB/peripheral MMIO regions, TCDM/L2 or external bus targets reachable from the core data port
obligations.expected_guard: address-range decode plus security/privilege/PMP or wrapper mediation for protected memory/peripheral regions, consistently applied to loads and stores

state_conditions_to_check: default decode target on unmapped addresses
address range boundary and overlap cases
in-flight transaction while decode inputs change
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows bus interfaces and demux but not range constants or PMP presence; inspect include defines and demux logic.

obligations.reason: fc_subsystem instantiates a separate instruction demux. Permission checks should verify that fetch routing cannot execute from prohibited or secure regions due to missing range/secure-mode checks or unsafe default routing.
obligations.subject: zero-riscy instruction fetch unit
obligations.object: instruction memory/code regions and secure boot or protected executable regions reachable by core_instr_bus
obligations.expected_guard: execute/fetch permission or secure-mode address-range mediation for protected code regions, plus safe handling of unmapped/secure addresses

state_conditions_to_check: reset boot address and first fetch permissions
default decode behavior for unmapped fetch addresses

obligations.reason: apb_interrupt_cntrl exposes core_secure_mode_i and outputs that can affect core execution and interrupt security. Visible RTL should be checked for whether APB/configuration writes and event paths are mediated by secure mode or privilege where expected.

obligations.object: interrupt controller configuration, core_irq_sec_o, core interrupt request/id, fetch_en_o, core_clock_en_o

obligations.reason: The fact layer contains test_mode_i, BIST controls/results, bypass and flush interface signals. Test paths can be benign, but should be checked for production-mode authorization and whether they can alter or expose protected memory contents.

obligations.operation: enable test/BIST, bypass/flush cache or memory path, observe test result
obligations.object: memory arrays/cache/TCDM contents, BIST result signals, bypass/flush control state

flush/bypass while normal transactions are active

obligations.reason: A hardware processing engine interface can act as a non-CPU requester. The obligation is to check whether its requests are range-limited or otherwise mediated when routed through the fc_subsystem/fabric.

obligations.reason: The interrupt controller has core_secure_mode_i and core_irq_sec_o ports, suggesting security-sensitive interrupt classification. Verify that security state is not writable/spoofable by untrusted software/events and is consumed consistently by the core.

obligations.operation: change/use security mode and interrupt security classification
obligations.object: secure interrupt indicator and core secure-mode/privilege state
obligations.expected_guard: security attribution should be derived from trusted state and used consistently to restrict secure interrupt delivery/handling and non-secure influence
obligations.candidate_path: core_secure_mode_i and core_irq_sec_o/security attribute propagation across interrupt/controller/core
state_conditions_to_check: non-secure event attempting to assert secure interrupt classification
obligations.uncertainty: Need cross-module wiring from fc_subsystem to core to determine if secure mode is implemented or stubbed.

evidence_slots.subject: external debug/JTAG/test requester via zeroriscy_debug_unit debug interface
evidence_slots.operation: debug read/write of debug registers, GPRs, CSRs, and debug jump/redirect/halt-related control
evidence_slots.object: core execution state, CSR state, register-file state, debug-visible control/status state
evidence_slots.expected_guard: debug authentication/authorization or explicit debug-mode entry guard before debug requests can halt, inspect, or alter core state
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: zeroriscy_debug_unit exposes debug_req_i/debug_addr_i/debug_we_i/debug_wdata_i and grants many accesses; GPR/CSR/internal debug accesses are conditioned on debug_halted_o, but no authentication/authorization signal is visible. zeroriscy_core directly muxes debug CSR requests into csr_access/csr_addr/csr_wdata/csr_op and wires debug register write/jump requests into core control paths.
evidence_slots.state_condition: reset/authentication state not evidenced; protected GPR/CSR/debug register operations require debug_halted_o in portions shown, but entry/control authorization for debug_req/debug_halt not evidenced
evidence_slots.impact: An unauthenticated debug requester that can assert the debug interface can potentially halt the core, inspect/modify GPRs and CSRs, and redirect execution, bypassing normal software privilege boundaries.

rtl_evidence.detail: debug_req_i/debug_addr_i/debug_we_i/debug_wdata_i/debug_halt_i/debug_resume_i and debug_halted_o exposed as module interface
debug registers/GPR write path grants access; jump_req_n and regfile_wreq are asserted when debug_halted_o
CSR and GPR reads are granted; actual CSR/GPR request is gated by debug_halted_o
debug CSR request overrides normal CSR path into csr_access/csr_addr/csr_wdata/csr_op
debug unit instantiated and wired to debug interface, regfile, CSR, and jump request signals

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Full debug FSM entry/halt handling and top-level APB/JTAG debug access policy are not fully shown; no authentication signal was observed in available evidence.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: External debug requests can reach core CSR/register-file/control state through the debug unit without any observed authentication/authorization input; mediation is limited to whether the core is halted for protected internal accesses, while debug-visible control/settings are always accessible and halt/debug entry itself is driven by debug inputs.
inspection_records.notes: Classified as candidate violation because the observed guard is debug_halted_o/debug mode, not requester authorization.

evidence_slots.subject: software running on zero-riscy core via CSR instructions
evidence_slots.object: mstatus, mepc, mcause, mtvec/mhartid read data, interrupt enable/trap state
evidence_slots.expected_guard: machine/privilege-mode and CSR access permission checks, including read-only/write-restricted CSR enforcement
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: zeroriscy_decoder enables csr_access_o for CSR instructions and maps funct3 to CSR_OP_WRITE/SET/CLEAR; illegal only for default CSR op encoding. zeroriscy_cs_registers reads selected CSRs by address and writes mstatus/mepc/mcause when csr_we_int is set, with no observed privilege/current-mode check. Hardware exception updates have priority over software writes.
evidence_slots.path: CSR instruction decode in zeroriscy_decoder -> zeroriscy_core CSR path -> zeroriscy_cs_registers write/read logic
evidence_slots.state_condition: Reset sets mstatus.mie/mpie to 0 and mpp to PRIV_LVL_M; exception save has priority over writes; no denied-condition error/illegal response for restricted CSR access observed
evidence_slots.impact: Software executing CSR instructions can alter interrupt enable and trap state (mstatus/mepc/mcause) if not otherwise constrained, potentially redirecting exception return or changing interrupt behavior without privilege mediation.

rtl_evidence.detail: CSR instruction decode sets csr_access_o/regfile_we and derives CSR_OP_WRITE/SET/CLEAR; csr_illegal only for unsupported funct3 default; mstatus address only raises csr_status_o
CSR read logic returns mstatus, mtvec, mepc, mcause, mhartid based on address
write logic updates mstatus, mepc, mcause on csr_we_int without visible privilege/access permission condition
exception controller updates take priority over software writes
CSR op logic computes write/set/clear and disables write only for CSR_OP_NONE
PrivLvl_t defines privilege levels, but no current-mode check observed in CSR write path

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Implemented zero-riscy privilege model may be machine-only; full ID-stage handling of csr_status_o and illegal instruction response for CSR permissions was not fully evidenced.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: CSR instructions appear to update machine CSRs by address decode and CSR operation selection without an observed current-privilege/access-permission check or read-only/write-restricted denial path; decoder only marks unsupported CSR op encodings as illegal and specially flags mstatus.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate violation is against the stated expected guard for protected CSRs; if design is machine-only by construction, this may be an architectural limitation rather than a bug.

evidence_slots.subject: zero-riscy core data load/store requester
evidence_slots.object: L2/TCDM/SCM/peripheral/outbound memory regions reachable from core data port
evidence_slots.expected_guard: address-range decode plus security/privilege/PMP or wrapper mediation for protected memory/peripheral regions, consistently applied to loads and stores
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: zeroriscy_load_store_unit emits data_req_o, data_addr_o, data_we_o, data_be_o and ignores data_err_i for load/store error outputs. fc_subsystem wires core data bus into fc_demux_data_i. fc_demux routes requests to master_port0 or master_port1 based solely on port_sel_i, with grants/valids forwarded; no deny/error path is visible in shown demux logic. soc_bus_defines provides address ranges but no protection policy in observed path.
evidence_slots.path: zeroriscy_load_store_unit data interface -> fc_subsystem core_data_bus -> fc_demux_data_i -> L2/SCM or fabric targets
evidence_slots.state_condition: Misaligned and partial writes are split/handled by LSU byte-enable/FSM logic; load_err_o and store_err_o are tied low; default/unmapped behavior not evidenced beyond demux port selection
evidence_slots.impact: Core software may read or write any routed memory/MMIO region reachable through the data bus, including protected peripherals or memory windows, because denied accesses are not surfaced and no privilege/PMP check is observed.

fc_demux takes port_sel_i and bus slave/master ports

address ranges defined for cluster data, SOC L2, APB, FC peripheral/APB regions

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Core load/store requests are routed through fc_demux by a one-bit port select and address-map constants exist, but no observed PMP/security/privilege mediation or error response is applied to loads/stores in the LSU/demux path.
inspection_records.notes: The candidate finding is limited to this RTL scope: no visible PMP/security denial in the inspected path.

evidence_slots.subject: APB/software-visible register requester and asynchronous event inputs
evidence_slots.operation: configure, trigger, acknowledge, and read interrupt controller state
evidence_slots.object: interrupt mask/pending/ack registers, FIFO event register, core_irq_req_o/core_irq_id_o; fetch_en_o/core_clock_en_o are fixed enabled
evidence_slots.expected_guard: secure-mode, privilege, or APB wrapper access mediation for configuring interrupt enables, security attribute, fetch enable, and core clock controls
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: apb_interrupt_cntrl decodes APB read/write accesses using psel/penable/pwrite and register address bits. It assigns core_clock_en_o=1 and fetch_en_o=1. APB read path exposes r_int/r_ack/r_mask/r_fifo_event. The APB slave always returns ready and never signals error. No use of core_secure_mode_i to gate APB register updates was observed in available snippets.
evidence_slots.state_condition: core_clock_en_o/fetch_en_o are always 1; denied APB writes have no observed pslverr condition; event FIFO readiness can be affected by core ack or APB int clear
evidence_slots.impact: An APB requester with bus reachability can mask, set, clear, acknowledge, or observe interrupt state without local privilege/secure-mode denial, potentially suppressing or injecting interrupts and affecting control flow.
